nameMeanings.name: d'Orival
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French and West Indian (mainly Haiti): habitational name, with fused preposition d(e) ‘from’, denoting someone from any of several places in France called Orival (from Latin aurea vallis ‘golden valley’), in Charente, Somme, and Seine-Maritime. This surname was brought to the US mostly from Haiti, where it is most common and where it may actually be an ornamental name (see Dorval ).

variants.name: Dorval
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Alain, Andre, Antoine, Armand, Emile, Marie-Ange, Micheline, Rosemonde, Solange, Sylvie.

Breton and French: habitational name, with fused French preposition d(e) ‘from’, for someone from either of two places called Orval (in Manche and Cher), the one in Manche recorded in Latin as Aurea Vallis ‘Golden Valley’ in 1100. In France, this surname is by far most common in Finistère in Brittany.
West Indian (mainly Haiti): nickname or ornamental name composed of French d'or ‘of gold’ and val ‘valley’. The surname may possibly also be of the same origin as 1 above.

nameMeanings.name: Mabilla
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Apparently an arbitrary elaboration of Maria , with the syllable -illa derived from names such as Priscilla .

en.lifeSummary: When Mabilla Aureval d'Orival was born in 1153, in Stanton Harcourt, Oxfordshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, Reginald Orvyle, was 44 and her mother, Muriel St John, was 44. She had at least 3 sons and 3 daughters with Adam Porter. She died in 1190, in Portchester, Hampshire, England, at the age of 37.

ko.lifeSummary: Mabilla Aureval d'Orival(이)가 1153년 영국 잉글랜드 스퍼드실르 스탠턴 하르쿠르트에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Reginald Orvyle(은)는 44세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Muriel St John(은)는 44세였다. 그녀는 Adam Porter(와)과 적어도 세 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1190년 영국 함프실르 포트체스터에서 37세의 나이로 사망했다.
